title: camelina rumelica subsp. rumelica
intro: Camelina rumelica subsp. rumelica, also known as Balkan false flax or Balkan false fieldcress, is a flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Camelina rumelica subsp. rumelica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it can also be used in the form of tincture or oil, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
